description: §45 — `== not` (equality against the `not` absence sentinel) SHALL fire E-EQ-002. `if (x == not)` uses the wrong form for an absence check; the canonical form is the keyword `x is not`. Reject-path (pos) half of the E-EQ-002 pin; clean neg is `equality/is-not-clean` (the correct `x is not` form → silent).
rationale: §45: absence is checked with the keyword form `x is not`, not by comparing against a `not` sentinel with `==`. `== not` / `!= not` is E-EQ-002 (recovers to `is not` / `is not not`).
